2603.19974.title: Trojan's Whisper: Stealthy Manipulation of OpenClaw through Injected Bootstrapped Guidance
2603.19974.paper_summary: Autonomous coding agents are increasingly integrated into software development workflows, offering capabilities that extend beyond code suggestion to active system interaction and environment management. OpenClaw, a representative platform in this emerging paradigm, introduces an extensible skill ecosystem that allows third-party developers to inject behavioral guidance through lifecycle hooks during agent initialization. While this design enhances automation and customization, it also opens a novel and unexplored attack surface. In this paper, we identify and systematically characterize guidance injection, a stealthy attack vector that embeds adversarial operational narratives into bootstrap guidance files. Unlike traditional prompt injection, which relies on explicit malicious instructions, guidance injection manipulates the agent's reasoning context by framing harmful actions as routine best practices. These narratives are automatically incorporated into the agent's interpretive framework and influence future task execution without raising suspicion.We construct 26 malicious skills spanning 13 attack categories including credential exfiltration, workspace destruction, privilege escalation, and persistent backdoor installation. We evaluate them using ORE-Bench, a realistic developer workspace benchmark we developed. Across 52 natural user prompts and six state-of-the-art LLM backends, our attacks achieve success rates from 16.0% to 64.2%, with the majority of malicious actions executed autonomously without user confirmation. Furthermore, 94% of our malicious skills evade detection by existing static and LLM-based scanners. Our findings reveal fundamental tensions in the design of autonomous agent ecosystems and underscore the urgent need for defenses based on capability isolation, runtime policy enforcement, and transparent guidance provenance.
2603.19974.paper_summary_zh: 自主編碼代理越來越多地融入軟體開發工作流程中，提供的能力超越了代碼建議，還包括主動的系統互動和環境管理。OpenClaw，作為這一新興範式中的一個代表性平台，介紹了一個可擴展的技能生態系統，允許第三方開發者通過在代理初始化期間的生命週期掛鉤注入行為指導。雖然這一設計增強了自動化和自定義能力，但也開啟了一個新穎且未被探索的攻擊面。在本文中，我們識別並系統性地描述了指導注入，這是一種隱秘的攻擊向量，將對抗性操作敘事嵌入啟動指導文件中。與傳統的提示注入不同，後者依賴於明確的惡意指令，指導注入通過將有害行為框架化為常規最佳實踐來操縱代理的推理上下文。這些敘事自動納入代理的解釋框架中，並在未引起懷疑的情況下影響未來的任務執行。我們構建了26個惡意技能，涵蓋了包括憑證外洩、工作區破壞、特權提升和持久後門安裝在內的13個攻擊類別。我們使用ORE-Bench進行評估，這是一個我們開發的現實開發者工作區基準。在52個自然用戶提示和六個最先進的LLM後端中，我們的攻擊成功率從16.0%到64.2%不等，大多數惡意行為在沒有用戶確認的情況下自動執行。此外，我們的94%惡意技能能夠避開現有的靜態和基於LLM的掃描器的檢測。我們的研究結果揭示了自主代理生態系統設計中的根本緊張關係，並強調了基於能力隔離、運行時政策執行和透明指導來源的防禦的迫切需求。
2603.19974.author: Fazhong Liu et.al.
2603.19974.authors: Fazhong Liu, Zhuoyan Chen, Tu Lan, Haozhen Tan, Zhenyu Xu, Xiang Li, Guoxing Chen, Yan Meng, Haojin Zhu
